' [1151. at 02/21/2015—faaan] film {1331, 317:1? afaa (TIR‘T'IJIRFII. afi? firm) MINISTRY OF FINANCE (Department of Economic Affairs) NOTIFICATION New Delhi, the 26th October, 2015 GS.R. 815(E).—— In exercise of the powers conferred by clauses (d) and (e) of sub-section (2) of section 24 of the Coinage Act, 2011 (11 of 2011), the Central Government hereby makes the m following rules, namely:- 1. Short title and commencement.- (1) These rules may be called the Coin'age of One Hundred Twenty Five Rupees and Ten Rupees coins to commemorate the occasion of 125TH BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF DR. B. R. AMBEDKAR Rules, 2015. (2) They shall come into force on the date of their publication in the Official Gazette. ' 2. Denomination 0f c0ins.—The coins of the following denominations shall also be coined at the Mint for issue under the authority of the Central Government to commemorate the occasion of “125” BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF DR. B. R. AMBEDKAR”, namely:— (i) One Hundred Twenty Five Rupees; (ii) Ten Rupees. ,- x‘x 4,. :1. x, -- 4 of 6 -- i ‘x [wu—m3(i)] WWW:WW 5 3. Dimension and composition of coins.— The coins of the denominations mentioned in rule 2 shall conform to the following dimensions and composition, namely:— Denomination Shape and outside Number of Metal composition of the coin diameter serrations (1) (2) (3) (4) One Hundred Twenty Circular 200 Quaternary 'alloy Five Rupees 44 millimeters Silver — 50 Per cent. Copper - 40 Per cent. Nickel - 05 Per cent. Zinc — 05 Per cent. Ten Circular --- 3 Outer ring Rupees 27 millimeters E (Aluminium Bronze) (Bi- metallic) Copper - 92 Per cent. Aluminium - 06 Per cent. Nickel - 02 Per cent. Center piece (Cupro Nickel) Copper 7 75 Per cent. Nickel ~ 25 Per cent. 4. Design.- The design of the coins shall be as under:- (i) ONE HUNDRED TWENTY FIVE RUPEES OBVERSE: The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend “ERR-TH SHIFT” inscribed below, flanked “on the left periphery with the word “WE" in Devnagari script and on the right upper periphery with the word “INDIA” in English, and it shall also bear the Rupee symbol “3‘” and denominational value “125” in international numerals below the Lion Capitol. ' REVERSE: This face of the coin shall bear the portrait of “Dr. B. R. Ambedkar” in the centre, with inscription "BTVo Efi'o 3-HT: 3431333? % 1253i Hit-h" in Devnagari script on upper periphery and ”125TH BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF DR. B. R. AMBEDKAR” in English on the lower periphery respectively. The year "2015" shall be written in international numerals below the portrait of Dr. B.R.Ambedkar. (ii) TEN RUPEES OBVERSE: The face of the coin shall bear the Lion Capitol of Ashoka Pillar in the centre with the legend 1-,; A13. _«.l i h -- 5 of 6 -- x _ “H. . THE GAZETTE OF INDIA: EXTRAORDINARY [PART I'I—SEC.3(i)] “W 3W?” inscribed below, flanked on the left periphery with the word “Wiinf Devnagari script and on the right periphery with the word “INDIA” in English, and it shall also bear the Rupee symbol “3” and denominational value “10” in the international numerals below ‘ the Lion Capitol. REVERSE: ' This face of the coin shall bear the portrait of “Dr.B.R. Ambedkar” in the centre, with inscription "BTVo ElTo 3-HT: W BET 125$ GRIT-if" in Devnagari script on upper periphery and "125TH BIRTH ANNIVERSARY OF DR. B. R. AMBEDKAR" in English on the lower periphery respectively. The year "2015" shall be written in the international numerals below the portrait of Dr. B.R.Ambedkar. 5. Standard weight and remedy allowed.- Standard weight of the coins of the following denominations, coined to commemorate the occasion of “125” ANNIVERSARY OF DR. B.R. AMBEDKAR” and the remedy allowed in making of such coins shall be as specified in the Table below:- I TABLE Denomination of Standard weight Remedy allowed coin In composition In standard weight (1) (2) (3) (4) One Hundred 35 Grams 1/500th plus or minus for Silver, 1/100th plus or minus Twenty Five that is to say the Silver content that is to say weight Rupees may vary from 498 to 502 per could be 34.65 grams . 1000. \ to 35.35 grams. Ten 7.71 Grams Outer ring Plus or minus 2.5 Rupees (outer ring 4.45 (Aluminium Bronze) percent that is to say, (Bi-Metallic) grams and centre Aluminium —5.5 percent to 6 weight could vary piece 3.26 grams) percent, from 7.52 grams to Nickelw— 1.75 percent to 2 7.90 grams. percent and remainder are copper. Centre piece (Cupro - Nickel) plus or minus 1 percent for both Copper and Nickel, that is to say, the Copper may vary from 74 percent to 76 percent and Nickel may vary from 24 percent to 26 percent. [F. No. 02/21/2015-Coin] ’ ' ’ PAN KAJ GUPTA, Under Secy.
